title: Red Sox continue to get bad news on Roman Anthony’s recovery from lingering wrist injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony hasn't played since May 4 due to a wrist injury, causing concern for the Red Sox.
Anthony's wrist progress remains slow; he's yet to advance to hitting with a regular bat.
Red Sox await Anthony's recovery from torn ligaments, typically taking 8-12 weeks to heal.

title: Celtics select St. John’s wing Dillon Mitchell with 40th pick in 2026 NBA Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics selected Dillon Mitchell from St. John's with the 40th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Dillon Mitchell averaged 8.3 points and 7.0 rebounds per game, earning All-Big East Third Team honors.
Mitchell, a strong defender with shooting limitations, played for Texas, Cincinnati, and St. John's in college.

title: Boy, 13, hospitalized after being found unresponsive in swimming pool at Beverly home
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 13-year-old boy was found unresponsive in a swimming pool at a home on Parramatta Road, Beverly.
Bystanders performed CPR on the boy until first responders arrived to continue life-saving measures.
The boy was stabilized at Beverly Hospital and then airlifted to a Boston hospital for further treatment.

title: Here’s where every tall ship will be docked for Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 250 will feature over 60 tall ships docked across Charlestown, downtown Boston, and the Seaport area.
Highlight vessels include Italy's Amerigo Vespucci, Argentina's Libertad, and Massachusetts's Ernestina-Morrissey accessible via Wharf 8.
Public viewings and boardings of ships run daily from July 12-15, with varying start times and captain discretion on visitor access.

title: Windhorst: ‘Very good chance’ Jaylen Brown isn’t on Celtics next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics are actively engaged in trade talks involving Jaylen Brown with several interested teams, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst speculates there's a 'very good chance' Jaylen Brown won't be with the Celtics next season.
Despite trade rumors, Celtics President Brad Stevens emphasized the value of Jaylen Brown but was noncommittal about his future with the team.

title: WCVB weeknight meteorologist Mike Wankum will retire end of June, station announces
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mike Wankum, WCVB's weeknight meteorologist, is set to retire on June 30 after over 20 years at Channel 5.
Cindy Fitzgibbon will take over Wankum's weeknight forecasts, with Kelly Ann Cicalese moving to weekdays for morning and noon.
Wankum, Boston's longest-serving broadcast meteorologist, earned 17 regional Emmy Awards and is a Certified Broadcast Meteorologist.

title: 14 years later, man charged in death of 7-year-old in Central Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Steven Stuart, 35, charged with murder in the 2017 death of Jayden Carlson, who died from complications of a 2012 assault.
Stuart, previously convicted in 2015 for assaulting Carlson, pleaded not guilty at arraignment in Worcester Superior Court.
Jayden suffered severe injuries in 2012 assault, requiring extensive medical care until his death at age 7 in Southbridge.

title: Is your favorite Boston bar packed with World Cup revelers? Here are some FIFA-free alternatives.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Grendel's Den in Cambridge offers a World Cup-free pub experience with no television.
Blossom Bar at Sichuan Garden in Brookline remains a peaceful retreat from sports, with minimal FIFA fan presence.
Long Bar & Terrace atop Boston's Raffles Hotel doesn't broadcast games and accepts reservations for a quiet escape.

title: Man allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from Medford church donation boxes, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Bagley, 60, allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from church donation boxes in Medford, police reported.
Bagley faces multiple charges, including larceny and breaking into a depository; he is suspected in similar thefts nearby.
He was arraigned in Somerville District Court, posted $100 bail, and is due back on August 26.

title: Celtics guard Derrick White is taking a basketball job with his alma mater at Colorado
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White named 'President of Basketball Strategy' at University of Colorado, supporting team development and culture.
White donates $2 million to Colorado's men's basketball, aiding revenue and program growth efforts.
As a Boston Celtic since 2021, White balances his NBA role with new duties at his alma mater.
